urgent_methodology: Publisher-marked Breaking, Urgent, Alert, Just in, Developing or similar items are collected first and receive priority over standard items, unless they are liveblogs, programme previews or low-value content.

latest.title: More people around the world now favour China over the US, Pew study suggests
latest.summary: China is now viewed more positively than the US in many countries around the world, according to a new study by the Pew Research Center. Pew polled more than 42,000 people in 36 countries between February and May, asking about their opinions of the two superpowers.
latest.details: China is now viewed more positively than the US in many countries around the world, according to a new study by the Pew Research Center. Pew polled more than 42,000 people in 36 countries between February and May, asking about their opinions of the two superpowers. The study found that in 25 of the 36 countries surveyed, more people had favourable views of China than of the US. Researchers noted that middle-income countries tended to have positive views of China, while wealthier nations were more critical. It is the first time the organisation has recorded such results. The findings from the non-partisan, US-based think tank indicate that favourable views of China have reached record highs in many countries, while perceptions of the United States have worsened.
latest.full_brief: China is now viewed more positively than the US in many countries around the world, according to a new study by the Pew Research Center. Pew polled more than 42,000 people in 36 countries between February and May, asking about their opinions of the two superpowers. The study found that in 25 of the 36 countries surveyed, more people had favourable views of China than of the US. Researchers noted that middle-income countries tended to have positive views of China, while wealthier nations were more critical. It is the first time the organisation has recorded such results. The findings from the non-partisan, US-based think tank indicate that favourable views of China have reached record highs in many countries, while perceptions of the United States have worsened.

latest.title: Seven Britons among those killed in Spain wildfires
latest.summary: Seven Britons were among 13 people killed in wildfires that swept through Spain's Almería province, authorities confirmed. Nearly 1,500 people were evacuated as the fires burned through approximately 7,000 hectares of land.
latest.details: Seven Britons were among 13 people killed in wildfires that swept through Spain's Almería province, authorities confirmed. Nearly 1,500 people were evacuated as the fires burned through approximately 7,000 hectares of land. Among the victims were British couple Pete and Fran Gillam, whose deaths were confirmed by their family. Spain has experienced frequent and severe heatwaves in recent years, with temperatures often exceeding 40°C. Twelve of the 13 victims were foreign nationals, Spanish authorities said on Tuesday after completing post-mortem examinations, according to local media and Agence France Presse (AFP). The wildfires, which tore through Spain's Almería province on Thursday, have become one of the country's deadliest.
latest.full_brief: Seven Britons were among 13 people killed in wildfires that swept through Spain's Almería province, authorities confirmed. Nearly 1,500 people were evacuated as the fires burned through approximately 7,000 hectares of land. Among the victims were British couple Pete and Fran Gillam, whose deaths were confirmed by their family. Spain has experienced frequent and severe heatwaves in recent years, with temperatures often exceeding 40°C. Twelve of the 13 victims were foreign nationals, Spanish authorities said on Tuesday after completing post-mortem examinations, according to local media and Agence France Presse (AFP). The wildfires, which tore through Spain's Almería province on Thursday, have become one of the country's deadliest.

latest.title: Air India crash report likely to be ready in October, say investigators
latest.summary: The Aircraft Accident Investigation Bureau (AAIB) of India has stated that a draft final report on the Air India crash that killed 260 people could be ready by October. The crash occurred on 12 June 2025, when an Air India Boeing 787 Dreamliner crashed seconds after takeoff from Ahmedabad airport, killing 241 passengers and 19 people on the ground.
latest.details: The Aircraft Accident Investigation Bureau (AAIB) of India has stated that a draft final report on the Air India crash that killed 260 people could be ready by October. The crash occurred on 12 June 2025, when an Air India Boeing 787 Dreamliner crashed seconds after takeoff from Ahmedabad airport, killing 241 passengers and 19 people on the ground. The AAIB has completed 49 of the 66 mandated steps for investigating serious aircraft accidents, including analyzing flight recorders and conducting psychological autopsies of crew members. The Aircraft Accident Investigation Bureau (AAIB) told the Supreme Court in an affidavit filed on Tuesday that it was currently "engaged in the analysis phase" of the investigation, which is expected to complete in around six weeks.
latest.full_brief: The Aircraft Accident Investigation Bureau (AAIB) of India has stated that a draft final report on the Air India crash that killed 260 people could be ready by October. The crash occurred on 12 June 2025, when an Air India Boeing 787 Dreamliner crashed seconds after takeoff from Ahmedabad airport, killing 241 passengers and 19 people on the ground. The AAIB has completed 49 of the 66 mandated steps for investigating serious aircraft accidents, including analyzing flight recorders and conducting psychological autopsies of crew members. The Aircraft Accident Investigation Bureau (AAIB) told the Supreme Court in an affidavit filed on Tuesday that it was currently "engaged in the analysis phase" of the investigation, which is expected to complete in around six weeks.

latest.title: UK begins trials of Ebola vaccine developed in just eight weeks
latest.summary: The vaccine targets the Bundibugyo species of Ebola, which has caused two previous outbreaks and is currently responsible for an epidemic in the Democratic Republic of the Congo. The trial will involve 50 healthy adults aged 18-55 in the UK, with researchers also preparing for trials in Africa.
latest.details: The vaccine targets the Bundibugyo species of Ebola, which has caused two previous outbreaks and is currently responsible for an epidemic in the Democratic Republic of the Congo. The trial will involve 50 healthy adults aged 18-55 in the UK, with researchers also preparing for trials in Africa. The vaccine uses technology from the Oxford/AstraZeneca Covid vaccine, employing a genetically modified chimpanzee cold virus to deliver a snippet of genetic code from the Ebola virus to trigger an immune response. Scientists at the University of Oxford started developing the vaccine eight weeks ago when a public health emergency was declared on 17 May. It is the first - out of four vaccines under development - to enter clinical trials. Volunteers are being recruited, with the first doses expected to be given to healthy adults in the UK "within weeks".
latest.full_brief: The vaccine targets the Bundibugyo species of Ebola, which has caused two previous outbreaks and is currently responsible for an epidemic in the Democratic Republic of the Congo. The trial will involve 50 healthy adults aged 18-55 in the UK, with researchers also preparing for trials in Africa. The vaccine uses technology from the Oxford/AstraZeneca Covid vaccine, employing a genetically modified chimpanzee cold virus to deliver a snippet of genetic code from the Ebola virus to trigger an immune response. Scientists at the University of Oxford started developing the vaccine eight weeks ago when a public health emergency was declared on 17 May. It is the first - out of four vaccines under development - to enter clinical trials. Volunteers are being recruited, with the first doses expected to be given to healthy adults in the UK "within weeks".

brief_methodology.pl: Jedna zasada: przeczytać dostępny tekst artykułu i streścić jego sens. Komentarz musi być ścisłym streszczeniem artykułu. Nie wolno przepisywać poleceń redakcyjnych typu „Skomentuj”, podpisów, źródeł, kategorii ani urwanych fragmentów.
brief_methodology.en: One rule: read the available article text and summarise its meaning. The comment must be a strict article summary. Do not copy editorial commands, bylines, source notes, categories or clipped fragments.

comment_quality_gate.pl: Publikowany jest wyłącznie komentarz utworzony z tekstu artykułu, zatwierdzony w niezależnym przeglądzie AI i przyjęty w całości przez rygorystyczną kontrolę językową. Jedno wadliwe zdanie odrzuca cały komentarz.
comment_quality_gate.en: Only an article-derived comment approved by an independent AI review and accepted in full by the strict language gate is published. One defective sentence rejects the whole comment.
